Notes: This is an example part! Add things to note here.
Servo for wrist roll and gripper
Ball bearing at the shoulder and wrist
Belt to connect belt gears on the shoulder
Servo motor driver that handles outputting all the PWM signals
Buck convertor to lower voltage from 12V down to 5V for esp32 and 6V for PCA9685
Capacitors will be used to prevent voltage spikes; this is for the servos. This is actually 5 pieces, although you just need 2
Power cord not included, so you would need one. Cutting up an old computer  power cord can also work
This is a 4-pack, but you only need 2. The fuses prevent short-circuit damage
Power distribution block lets everything be more organized, it's optional since wires can be plugged directly into the power supply
Thicker wires can safely handle the 12V 20A power without any fluctuation or trouble, you would also need regular jumper wires
This part controls the stepper motor
This capacitor will be used for the stepper motor. This is 10 pieces but you only need 1

Item Name: All of these parts are just the ones I found immediately on Amazon; you can probably find the exact same parts for cheaper on Amazon or even somewhere else.
